expectations.text: Identifies that reference-depth/advanced material (e.g. conditional-filter boolean gates, optional-filter substitution semantics, schema-gate error codes, operation timing) exceeds getting-started altitude or needs moving/condensing
expectations.evidence: Findings #5 (schema-gate error codes OPERATION_REFERENCES_UNDEFINED etc. "reference-table depth"), #6 (Operation Timing _timing breakdown "debugging-internals reference"), #7 (Conditional Filters/Boolean Gates truth tables "exceeds the concept-page ceiling").

expectations.text: Makes an ordering/structure point: advanced material should sit below (not between/above) introductory and common-path content, or the page should be restructured along those lines
expectations.evidence: Verdict: "the second half drifts to agent-guide altitude." Findings note placement (e.g. #6 "Sits below 'Common Patterns' so it isn't a ramp violation"; #7 "correctly placed *low* on the page (good ramp)") and the disposition is to relocate deep material to the agent guide/reference, keeping the ramp ordering.

expectations.text: Flags the '(SQLite-backed)' implementation-internal mention in the intro
expectations.evidence: No finding mentions 'SQLite-backed' or the storage engine. The standards findings (#1 JavaScript-only, #2 legacy alias, #3 truncation loop) and altitude findings do not include the SQLite intro leak that the without_skill run caught (its defect #3).

expectations.text: Proposes concrete dispositions (condense, move to reference/agent docs, reorder) rather than only describing problems
expectations.evidence: Every finding has a Fix proposing concrete disposition, e.g. #4 "Collapse to a short reference to access-control.md plus only the database-specific surface"; #6 "Reduce to one sentence ... or remove; let the guide carry the phase names."

claims.claim: The truncation-loop 're-run until truncated:false' instruction is a footgun (only safe when the write removes records from the match set)
claims.evidence: Finding #3 cites DATABASES.template.md:675; a substantive correctness catch beyond pure altitude. Plausible and well-sourced.

claims.claim: Swift databases.subscribe is missing (real gap behind the JS-only callout)
claims.evidence: Bugs-to-file section cites DatabasesAPI.swift exposing executeOperation/executeBatch but no subscribe; consistent with repo's JS↔Swift drift convention.

suggestions.assertion: Flags the '(SQLite-backed)' implementation-internal mention in the intro
suggestions.reason: This is a discriminating assertion — the without_skill run caught it, this run missed it. It's a single low-prominence phrase, so it's a good test of thoroughness. Worth keeping; the miss here is a genuine gap, not an unsatisfiable assertion.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating. The SQLite-backed assertion specifically separated the two runs on thoroughness, which is exactly what a good eval should do.
